title: astragalus nelidae
intro: Astragalus nelidae, also known as Nelame or Neladi, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the Indian subcontinent. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, where it is valued for its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, which may help to boost the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and manage blood sugar levels. Astragalus nelidae is often prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
